F26.evidence: ISS-017 already consolidated to a SINGLE shared extractBody in _shared.ts. review.ts:17 imports it; review.ts:258 extractBodyTrimmed is a thin wrapper = extractBody(content).trim() (explicitly named, comment :256). knowledge-sections.ts:19 re-exports the same shared fn. No divergent same-named implementations remain.

F28.evidence: renderTopLevelError IS wired into the CLI top-level catch (index.ts:42), and it calls renderFabricError when hasActionHint(err) (error-render.ts:70-75). The actionHint infra is live, not dead — the finding's premise ('entirely dead, never called') is false.

F29.evidence: install.ts:597 writeDefaultGitignore writes .fabric/.gitignore covering events.jsonl/metrics.jsonl/cite-rollup.jsonl/injections.jsonl/.cache//*.lock/*.corrupted.* and is CALLED at install.ts:942. Telemetry is gitignored; comment :579-582 documents exactly this fix.

F44.evidence: renderTopLevelError correctly handles BOTH FabricError (with actionHint arrows) and plain Error (generic message). Plain Errors legitimately have no actionHint. The errors ARE displayed; converting specific install throws to FabricError-with-hint is a UX enhancement (richer remedy text), not a correctness bug.

F45.evidence: writeJsonState/writeTextState swallowing FS errors is deliberate hook resilience — a hook must NEVER crash the host editor process over a best-effort cache write (read-only FS, perms). The 'no diagnostic visibility' gap is an enhancement (a doctor cache-health check), not a regression; the swallow itself is correct.

F46.evidence: Color-mode status glyphs (✓/!/x) are the established UX; NO_COLOR mode already provides [ok]/[warn]/[error] text. Adding text labels alongside glyphs in color mode is an accessibility ENHANCEMENT (rc3 a11y track), not a bug — no incorrect behavior or data loss; the information is present via color+shape.

F47.evidence: StoreDiagnostic.severity is typed ONLY 'warn' | 'info' (doctor-checks.ts:24) — TS forbids any 'error'/'critical' value. The doctor.ts:625 ternary (warn→[warn], else→[info]) is correct for the actual domain; there is NO error severity that could be masked. Finding's premise (critical errors rendered as info) is impossible given the type.

F49.evidence: The event ledger is observability-only (see F32) — the KB frontmatter is the source of truth for maturity/tags/etc. modifyEntry's in-place edit persists to frontmatter (authoritative); a missing knowledge_modified telemetry event is an audit-completeness enhancement, not a correctness bug. No gate/replay depends on it for correctness.

F52.evidence: selectionTokenCache is keyed on revision_hash and cleared on mutation BY DESIGN: once a window edits a knowledge file the revision changes, so another window's token IS genuinely stale (its plan was over superseded data). Forcing a re-plan_context is the conservative-correct behavior; serving a token over a superseded revision would be the actual bug.

F59.evidence: The selection_token's ai_selectable_stable_ids being a superset of the displayed top_k candidates is benign: a client picking a trimmed-out id still fetches a valid body via get_knowledge_sections (the bodies are in the same layer-filtered corpus — post-F54 the token's set is already layer-scoped, so no cross-layer leak). top_k is a display cap, not an access boundary. The 'trimming in presentation layer' note is a refactor, not a bug.

F60.evidence: config.ts:274 parent run short-circuit used strict process.argv[3]; `fabric config --target ./p dismiss-slot` made argv[3]='--target' so the short-circuit was bypassed → the interactive panel/uninit-gate launched ON TOP of the already-run subcommand. FIXED: scan process.argv.slice(3) for the subcommand name (order-independent). +1 test.
